Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"As a carpenter who spends a few times monthly on social media, I've noticed how quickly false information can spread through these platforms. Managing and dealing with fake content requires patience and a proactive approach. Firstly, it’s essential to verify any suspicious information before sharing. This means checking multiple credible sources to ensure accuracy. My father, who was always meticulous in his work, would say that double-checking is key to quality craftsmanship, and the same principle applies here. Secondly, I find it helpful to engage with social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools or report features. This not only helps me personally but also contributes to a more reliable online environment for everyone. Sharing verified content can set a good example for friends and family, many of whom are new to social media and might not always recognize the importance of verifying information.","reasoning_summary":"Starting with the necessity of verification, I rely on my background in craftsmanship to stress the value of checking sources thoroughly before sharing any content online. This is akin to ensuring that the materials used in carpentry meet high standards. My persona’s high extraversion means they are comfortable engaging with others but might not always prioritize fact-checking. By highlighting how verifying information aligns with their proactive nature, I aim to make it more appealing. The use of social media platforms' built-in tools also leverages the persona's reliance on technology while maintaining practicality.","tone":"Supportive and practical.","citations":["<none>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["extraversion"]}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a carpenter who spends a few times monthly on social media, I've noticed how quickly false information can spread through these platforms. Managing and dealing with fake content requires patience and a proactive approach. Firstly, it’s essential to verify any suspicious information before sharing. This means checking multiple credible sources to ensure accuracy. My father, who was always meticulous in his work, would say that double-checking is key to quality craftsmanship, and the same principle applies here. Secondly, I find it helpful to engage with social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools or report features. This not only helps me personally but also contributes to a more reliable online environment for everyone. Sharing verified content can set a good example for friends and family, many of whom are new to social media and might not always recognize the importance of verifying information.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Starting with the necessity of verification, I rely on my background in craftsmanship to stress the value of checking sources thoroughly before sharing any content online. This is akin to ensuring that the materials used in carpentry meet high standards. My persona’s high extraversion means they are comfortable engaging with others but might not always prioritize fact-checking. By highlighting how verifying information aligns with their proactive nature, I aim to make it more appealing. The use of social media platforms' built-in tools also leverages the persona's reliance on technology while maintaining practicality.
